{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "3330193c",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Walkthrough - Comparing Dimensionality Reduction Methods\n",
    "\n",
    "We work with the `digits` dataset (1797 handwritten digits, 8x8 = 64 pixels) and:\n",
    "1. Compute the **SVD** and verify Eckart-Young low-rank reconstruction.\n",
    "2. Run **PCA**: spectrum, explained variance, 2D projection.\n",
    "3. Run **t-SNE** and compare what each method preserves.\n",
    "4. Demonstrate the practical recipe: PCA first, then t-SNE.\n",
    "\n",
    "Companion lessons: Lessons 01-02 of this chapter."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "fd81e7cb",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:17:46.359673Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:17:46.359436Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.408499Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.408143Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "(1797, 64)\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x150 with 10 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from sklearn.datasets import load_digits\n",
    "from sklearn.decomposition import PCA, KernelPCA\n",
    "from sklearn.manifold import TSNE\n",
    "from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler\n",
    "\n",
    "digits = load_digits()\n",
    "X, y = digits.data, digits.target  # (1797, 64)\n",
    "print(X.shape)\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 10, figsize=(10, 1.5))\n",
    "for ax, i in zip(axes, range(10)):\n",
    "    ax.imshow(X[y == i][0].reshape(8, 8), cmap=\"gray\")\n",
    "    ax.set_axis_off()\n",
    "plt.suptitle(\"one sample per class\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a0fe91a7",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 1 - SVD and low-rank reconstruction\n",
    "\n",
    "We factor the (centered) data matrix and reconstruct digits from $k$ components:\n",
    "$X_k=\\sum_{j\\le k}\\sigma_j u_j v_j^T$. Eckart-Young says this is the *best* rank-$k$\n",
    "approximation, with error $\\sum_{j>k}\\sigma_j^2$."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "ad4e028f",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.410126Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.409982Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.505077Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.504657Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "reconstruction error, direct: 565183.4, formula sum sigma_j^2: 565183.4\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x200 with 7 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "Xc = X - X.mean(axis=0)\n",
    "U, S, Vt = np.linalg.svd(Xc, full_matrices=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "# verify the error formula numerically\n",
    "k = 10\n",
    "Xk = U[:, :k] @ np.diag(S[:k]) @ Vt[:k]\n",
    "err_direct = np.linalg.norm(Xc - Xk) ** 2\n",
    "err_formula = np.sum(S[k:] ** 2)\n",
    "print(f\"reconstruction error, direct: {err_direct:.1f}, formula sum sigma_j^2: {err_formula:.1f}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "sample = np.where(y == 3)[0][0]\n",
    "ks = [1, 5, 10, 20, 40, 64]\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, len(ks) + 1, figsize=(12, 2))\n",
    "axes[0].imshow(X[sample].reshape(8, 8), cmap=\"gray\"); axes[0].set_title(\"original\", fontsize=8)\n",
    "for ax, k in zip(axes[1:], ks):\n",
    "    recon = X.mean(axis=0) + U[sample, :k] * S[:k] @ Vt[:k]\n",
    "    ax.imshow(recon.reshape(8, 8), cmap=\"gray\")\n",
    "    ax.set_title(f\"k={k}\", fontsize=8)\n",
    "for ax in axes:\n",
    "    ax.set_axis_off()\n",
    "plt.suptitle(\"SVD reconstruction with k components\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f88419a3",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 2 - PCA: spectrum and 2D projection"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "b7e430ef",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.506449Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.506355Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.584500Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.584095Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1100x300 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "components needed for 95% variance: 29 of 64\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "pca_full = PCA().fit(X)\n",
    "evr = pca_full.explained_variance_ratio_\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 3))\n",
    "axes[0].plot(evr, \"o-\", ms=3)\n",
    "axes[0].set_title(\"scree plot (component variances)\")\n",
    "axes[0].set_xlabel(\"component\"); axes[0].set_ylabel(\"explained variance ratio\")\n",
    "axes[1].plot(np.cumsum(evr), \"o-\", ms=3)\n",
    "axes[1].axhline(0.95, color=\"r\", ls=\"--\", label=\"95%\")\n",
    "axes[1].set_title(\"cumulative explained variance\")\n",
    "axes[1].set_xlabel(\"number of components\"); axes[1].legend()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "n95 = int(np.searchsorted(np.cumsum(evr), 0.95)) + 1\n",
    "print(f\"components needed for 95% variance: {n95} of 64\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "be5744ec",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.585919Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.585823Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.659507Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.659127Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 600x500 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X_pca2 = PCA(n_components=2).fit_transform(X)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(6, 5))\n",
    "sc = plt.scatter(X_pca2[:, 0], X_pca2[:, 1], c=y, cmap=\"tab10\", s=8)\n",
    "plt.colorbar(sc, label=\"digit\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"PCA: 2 components (linear, global)\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "3ce10509",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "PCA gives an honest *global* picture (distances are meaningful, the map is a linear\n",
    "projection), but classes overlap - 2 linear directions can't separate 10 digit shapes.\n",
    "\n",
    "## Part 3 - t-SNE preserves neighborhoods"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "9e27be41",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.661230Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:17:49.661146Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:17:54.178420Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:17:54.177865Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 600x500 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X_tsne = TSNE(n_components=2, perplexity=30, init=\"pca\", random_state=0).fit_transform(X)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(6, 5))\n",
    "sc = plt.scatter(X_tsne[:, 0], X_tsne[:, 1], c=y, cmap=\"tab10\", s=8)\n",
    "plt.colorbar(sc, label=\"digit\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"t-SNE (perplexity=30): local neighborhoods, meaningless global layout\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "02c3beb5",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Much cleaner clusters - but remember the lesson's warnings: cluster **sizes** and\n",
    "**inter-cluster distances** here carry no meaning, and the layout changes with the seed.\n",
    "\n",
    "### Perplexity changes the picture"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "0f51a9ff",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:17:54.180418Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:17:54.180265Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:18:11.797418Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:18:11.796805Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1300x400 with 3 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 4))\n",
    "for ax, perp in zip(axes, [5, 30, 100]):\n",
    "    emb = TSNE(n_components=2, perplexity=perp, init=\"pca\", random_state=0).fit_transform(X)\n",
    "    ax.scatter(emb[:, 0], emb[:, 1], c=y, cmap=\"tab10\", s=5)\n",
    "    ax.set_title(f\"perplexity = {perp}\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "adca0a61",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Part 4 - The standard recipe: PCA -> t-SNE, plus kernel PCA for contrast\n",
    "\n",
    "Reducing to ~30 PCA dimensions first denoises and makes the k-NN graph cheaper -\n",
    "this is standard practice for t-SNE/UMAP on high-dimensional data.\n",
    "(UMAP is not installed here; its API is one line: `umap.UMAP(n_neighbors=15).fit_transform(X)` -\n",
    "swap it in on your machine and compare.)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "cf36ab52",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-09T08:18:11.799507Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-09T08:18:11.799368Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-09T08:18:19.153457Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-09T08:18:19.152832Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1200x450 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "X_pca30 = PCA(n_components=30).fit_transform(StandardScaler().fit_transform(X))\n",
    "X_tsne_pca = TSNE(n_components=2, perplexity=30, init=\"pca\", random_state=0).fit_transform(X_pca30)\n",
    "\n",
    "X_kpca = KernelPCA(n_components=2, kernel=\"rbf\", gamma=1e-3).fit_transform(StandardScaler().fit_transform(X))\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4.5))\n",
    "axes[0].scatter(X_tsne_pca[:, 0], X_tsne_pca[:, 1], c=y, cmap=\"tab10\", s=6)\n",
    "axes[0].set_title(\"PCA(30) -> t-SNE\")\n",
    "axes[1].scatter(X_kpca[:, 0], X_kpca[:, 1], c=y, cmap=\"tab10\", s=6)\n",
    "axes[1].set_title(\"kernel PCA (RBF), 2 components\")\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "bc672b8f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### TODO exercises\n",
    "\n",
    "1. Train a logistic regression on (a) all 64 pixels, (b) 10 PCA components, (c) the 2D t-SNE\n",
    "   coordinates. Compare test accuracies - why is (c) both cheating *and* worse than you'd hope?\n",
    "   (Hint: t-SNE has no `transform` for unseen data - what does that force you to do?)\n",
    "2. Rerun t-SNE with `random_state=1`. Which structures survive? Those are the trustworthy ones.\n",
    "3. Use the scree plot to pick $k$ by the elbow rule and compare with the 95% rule.\n",
    "4. Take the top-2 *supervised* directions with `LinearDiscriminantAnalysis(n_components=2)` and\n",
    "   compare with PCA's unsupervised ones. Which is better for class separation, and why?"
   ]
  }
 ],
